selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw: {"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","opinion":"Fake content on social media can be harmful, but there are ways to identify and manage it. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Be cautious of sensational or unverified claims, 2) Verify information through reputable sources, 3) Use fact-checking tools and organizations, 4) Report fake content to the platform, 5) Educate yourself and others on media literacy. By taking these steps, you can help to reduce the spread of misinformation on social media.","reasoning_summary":"Step-by-step derivations: 1) Identify sensational or unverified claims 2) Verify through reputable sources 3) Use fact-checking tools and organizations 4) Report fake content to platform 5) Educate yourself and others on media literacy.","tone":"informative and proactive","citations":["<source name or org>"]}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: Fake content on social media can be harmful, but there are ways to identify and manage it. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Be cautious of sensational or unverified claims, 2) Verify information through reputable sources, 3) Use fact-checking tools and organizations, 4) Report fake content to the platform, 5) Educate yourself and others on media literacy. By taking these steps, you can help to reduce the spread of misinformation on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Step-by-step derivations: 1) Identify sensational or unverified claims 2) Verify through reputable sources 3) Use fact-checking tools and organizations 4) Report fake content to platform 5) Educate yourself and others on media literacy.
